parsed_review.summary: CP00-754 (RP24.P09.M06.S03-S12, risk A, 10 units, descriptor-only "Synthetic Fixture Set" slice) passes read-only review. Directly verified by reading source: the CP754 pack binding/requirements/descriptor/case-set chain (registry.js:553,4407; service.js:129-130) correctly extends CP00-753 with next_pack CP00-755 and runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-755; the required-section-rows slice KOREAN_LEGAL_CP752_REVIEW_CLOSEOUT_ROWS.slice(2,12) maps exactly to the 10 S03-S12 unit titles in the baseline; the deliverable distribution (security_audit:2,test:1,ui:1,implementation:6) matches baseline and requirements; three CP754 tests (model.test.js:73-75) cover binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and latest contract projection; validators.js:29-30 enforce no-leak guards, descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-sensitive-payload rows, the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false), and gate/handoff consistency; the regenerated contract sets cp754 as latest_pack/latest_projection while preserving cp722-cp753; the validator script (validate-rp24-korean-legal-depth-contract.mjs:44) includes the cp754 entry; README.md:133 and package.json scripts are present. The pack-level claim is production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false (status in_progress), so no premature production/runtime/enterprise-trust claim is made; the descriptor-layer flag remains pending this review. Boundaries are intact: Hermes is used only for deterministic packet/receipt handling (emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write), no real client/matter/legal data, no product-state write, and Claude stays read-only and non-final. Review-hardening checks: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1469-byte valid JSON result), and the forbidden_review_evidence set (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose) is encoded as invalid_review_blockers; the claude-review-result is correctly in the pending pre-review state. PROVENANCE NOTE: as a read-only reviewer I did not re-execute the validation ladder; the 25 commands at exit 0 (incl. npm test, closeout-pack:validate, weighted/fullplan/goal/control-plane validates, git diff --check) are taken from the recorded command-evidence.json, not independently re-run. One P3 cosmetic finding: the changed-file-scope.json helper double-classifies the pack's own evidence directory and the session-handoff file as both active-untracked-implementation and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, diverging from the (correct) manifest categorization; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty so no active file is wrongly dropped from scope. Non-blocking for CP00-754 closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own evidence directory as unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-754/ is the pack's own required evidence directory: its member files are enumerated in active_pack_required_untracked_files (lines 17-23) and the directory is listed in untracked_implementation_files (line 182). It is simultaneously listed in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 190), and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md shows the same overlap (lines 181 and 188). This contradicts the manifest's worktree_transition_policy (manifest.json:194-214), which correctly lists the cp00-754 artifacts only under active_pack_required_untracked_files and does NOT place them under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. The safety-critical guard active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (also confirmed by validation-report.json:22), so no active implementation file is wrongly omitted from scope; this is a non-authoritative metadata overlap in a generated review-scope helper, not a defect in the pack's committed deliverables, and does not affect the production_ready determination, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, or what is staged at closeout.
findings.recommendation: Update the review-baseline generator so that active required/implementation untracked paths (the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<id>/ directory and the session-handoff file) are excluded from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, making changed-file-scope.json match the manifest's worktree_transition_policy categorization. No pack content or revalidation change is required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Cosmetic metadata overlap in a generated review-scope helper; does not affect production_ready evidence, authority/no-write boundaries, or closeout staging. Recommend generator dedup in a future tooling pass.
